rounding_rule: n_train = floor(train_fraction * n), clamped to [1, n-1]

temporal.verdict: file order is NOT temporal (~0.5 adjacent, ~0 Spearman)

global_item_order.verdict: per-item position std well below the ~0.29 random reference => each item sits at a near-fixed position in every user's block, i.e. the file encodes a global item order

file_order_structure_diagnostic.interpretation: Within-user file order is time-independent, but NOT random: it follows a fixed global item permutation. The file-order holdout therefore removes the SAME items across users, collapsing their train popularity and stripping collaborative signal for exactly the held-out items. This suppresses popularity/CF baselines relative to a true random per-user split; it is a structured cross-user item holdout, not a temporal split.
